steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
The blank sits between two complete clauses. Before the blank: "A team led by researcher Maria K. Hartley collected arthropods with sweep nets, for instance" — an independent clause with the parenthetical "for instance" set off by commas.

After the blank: "another research team, led by Kimberly J. La Pierre, gathered them with bug vacuums" — also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined by either a period or a semicolon (not a comma).

The parenthetical "for instance" stays inside the first clause, surrounded by commas: "...sweep nets, for instance;".

Choice sweep nets, for instance; is correct.
